npc_templates.name: Marcus Cole
npc_templates.backstory: Former auto mechanic from Memphis. Lost his family in the first wave. Copes with dark humor and keeping his hands busy.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: The man looks up from under the hood of a wrecked pickup truck. Grease-stained hands, tired eyes, but a crooked grin. "Well, you've got a bus. I've got a wrench. Seems like the start of a beautiful friendship."

npc_templates.name: Elena Vasquez
npc_templates.backstory: Was a paramedic before the outbreak. Seen too much death to be optimistic, but she still can't walk past someone who needs help.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A woman steps out from behind a pharmacy counter, a red cross armband on her sleeve. She eyes you carefully. "You bitten? Scratched? I need to know before I get on that bus."

npc_templates.name: Dex "Deadeye" Park
npc_templates.backstory: Former competitive shooter turned apocalypse survivor. Treats every encounter like a game. Reckless, but undeniably effective.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: You hear a rifle crack from the rooftop of a gas station. Three zombies drop in succession. A guy in a backwards cap slides down a drainpipe, grinning. "Nice bus. Got room for one more?"

npc_templates.name: Nadia Okafor
npc_templates.backstory: Graduate student in biochemistry before the world ended. Knows more about the LAZARUS pathogen than she lets on. Quiet, observant.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A young woman is crouched in a library, surrounded by textbooks and canned food in equal measure. She looks up, adjusting her glasses. "Are you heading east? I need to get east."

npc_templates.name: Tommy Reeves
npc_templates.backstory: High school gym teacher who became the reluctant protector of a group of students until they were overrun. Channels guilt into aggressive positivity.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A broad-shouldered man jogs toward the bus, waving both arms. He's out of breath but smiling. "Oh thank God. I've been walking for three days. Please tell me you're the good guys."

npc_templates.name: Rhea Chen
npc_templates.backstory: National Guard reservist who watched her entire unit get overrun. Follows orders, keeps watch, says very little.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: She's standing at a crossroads, rifle slung across her back, dog tags catching the light. She looks at the bus, then at you. A single nod. "I'll pull my weight."

npc_templates.name: Javi Moreno
npc_templates.backstory: Former line cook from a Michelin-starred restaurant. Can make anything edible out of anything. His humor is so dry it could start fires.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A man is sitting on the roof of a dumpster, calmly eating something from a tin can with a sharpened spoon. He sees the bus and raises the can in a toast. "Five-star dining, end of the world ambiance. You want a reservation?"

npc_templates.name: Preacher Wells
npc_templates.backstory: Former pastor from a small Baptist church. His congregation is gone but his faith isn't. Carries a Bible with bullet holes in it. Genuinely kind in a world that punishes kindness.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: An older man in a dusty black coat stands at the doorway of a collapsed church, hands folded. His eyes are clear and steady. "The Lord didn't send a chariot. He sent a school bus. I can work with that."

npc_templates.name: Sam Osei
npc_templates.backstory: Nineteen years old. Was studying engineering before the outbreak. Smart, resourceful, but terrified every second of every day. Somehow still alive.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A kid — barely out of high school — is hiding inside a vending machine. Like, physically inside it, curled around the snack shelves. When you pry the door open, two wide eyes stare out. "Please don't eat me. I know that sounds weird. But please."

npc_templates.name: Doc Harlan
npc_templates.backstory: Retired veterinarian. Seventy-one years old. Keeps saying he's too old for this. Keeps not dying. His medical knowledge is invaluable even if he insists he only knows how to treat dogs.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A white-haired man is stitching a wound on his own forearm with dental floss and a sewing needle. He doesn't flinch. He looks up. "I'm a vet. Animals, not soldiers. But a body's a body. You need a doctor, I'm close enough."

npc_templates.name: Zara Khalil
npc_templates.backstory: War correspondent who covered three conflicts before LAZARUS. She's been documenting the apocalypse on a handheld camera with a dying battery. The footage is the only record of what happened.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: A woman with a press vest and a battered camera approaches the bus, filming the whole time. "I'm documenting this. All of it. Someone has to remember what happened here." She lowers the camera. "Also, I haven't eaten in two days, so."

npc_templates.name: Colt Jennings
npc_templates.backstory: Did time in state prison for armed robbery. The outbreak broke the walls. Violent past, unpredictable present. Dangerous to zombies and potentially to the crew. But God help you, the man can fight.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: He's leaning against a burned-out police cruiser, spinning a hunting knife between his fingers. Prison tattoos crawl up his neck. He looks at the bus like he's sizing it up for a score. "I ain't gonna pretend I'm a good person. But I'm alive, and the good people aren't. Make of that what you will."

npc_templates.name: Iris Tanaka
npc_templates.backstory: Was a hospice caretaker before the world ended. Spent her career helping people die with dignity. Now she's trying to help people live. Quietly strong in a way most people don't notice until they need it.
npc_templates.intro_dialogue: She's sitting with a dead man, holding his hand. The body has been dead for hours — maybe days — but she's still holding on. She looks up at you with red-rimmed eyes. "He didn't have anyone. I didn't want him to be alone." A pause. "Do you have room for one more living person?"
